message:
feat(pager): implement D1-CRITICAL split-lock commit protocol (bd-3wop3.8)

This commit implements Change 1 of the D1-CRITICAL contention fix, which
splits the inner.lock() held during commit into three phases to enable
parallel prepare phases across threads.

## Problem

The commit() function held inner.lock() for the ENTIRE commit duration
(~100us), serializing all 16 threads. At 16 threads: 16 × 100us = 1.6ms
serialized per batch, causing 0.81x throughput vs C SQLite.

## Solution: Split-Lock Protocol

BEFORE: inner.lock() held for entire commit (~100us)
AFTER:
  - Phase A (prepare, ~20us): Hold inner.lock() briefly for state snapshot
  - DROP inner.lock() <-- Thread B can now start its Phase A
  - Phase B (WAL I/O, ~50us): Acquire consolidator.lock(), then briefly
    inner.lock() for actual I/O
  - Phase C (publish, ~10us): Re-acquire inner.lock() for finalization

## Changes

1. commit_wal_group_commit(): Changed signature from `&mut PagerInner`
   to `&Arc<Mutex<PagerInner>>`. Function now acquires inner.lock()
   internally AFTER acquiring consolidator.lock(), enabling the
   split-lock protocol.

2. commit(): Added D1-CRITICAL comment block. For WAL mode: drops
   inner after Phase A, passes Arc to commit_wal_group_commit, then
   re-acquires inner for Phase C.

3. commit_and_retain(): Same split-lock pattern applied.

4. SharedWalBackend infrastructure: Added type alias and constructor
   for future use in full WAL backend separation.

## Expected Impact

- Parallelizes ~20us prepare phase across N threads
- Estimated 2-3x improvement at 16 threads
- Foundation for Change 2 (real group commit batching)

## Lock Ordering (Prevents Deadlock)

1. Acquire consolidator.lock() — serializes WAL I/O
2. Acquire inner.lock() — briefly, just for I/O
3. Do WAL I/O
4. Release inner.lock()
5. Release consolidator.lock()

While holding consolidator.lock(), other threads can acquire inner.lock()
and run their prepare phase, then queue behind consolidator.lock().

Also includes: D6 seqlock test formatting fixes (import ordering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
